IsA: It is a series of 24 fresco panels.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by themes of the impact of indigenous Native Americans and European colonists on North America.
It is characterized by themes of the impact of war on the human spirit.
It is characterized by themes of the impact of rapid industrialization on the human spirit.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting the impact of indigenous Native Americans and European colonists on North America.
It is used for depicting the impact of war on the human spirit.
It is used for depicting the impact of rapid industrialization on the human spirit.
LocatedIn: It is located in the basement reading room of the Baker Memorial Library.
It is located on the campus of Dartmouth College.
It is located in Hanover, New Hampshire.
CreatedBy: It was created by the social realist painter José Clemente Orozco.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a series of 24 fresco panels.
PartOf: It is part of the Baker Memorial Library.
It is part of Dartmouth College.
KnownFor: It is known for its themes of the impact of indigenous Native Americans and European colonists on North America.
It is known for its themes of the impact of war on the human spirit.
It is known for its themes of the impact of rapid industrialization on the human spirit.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with social realism.
It is associated with José Clemente Orozco.
It is associated with Dartmouth College.
It is associated with Ernest Hopkins.
It is associated with the Mexican Civil War.
It is associated with the First World War.
About: It is about the impact of indigenous Native Americans and European colonists on North America.
It is about the impact of war on the human spirit.
It is about the impact of rapid industrialization on the human spirit.
It is about the arrival of natives as a grim march.
It is about human sacrifice as practiced by ancient Mesoamericans.
It is about Aztec warriors wearing eagle and jaguar costumes.
It is about Quetzalcoatl as a benevolent god.
It is about the benefits of agriculture, arts, and science brought by Quetzalcoatl.
It is about adherents of banished gods driving Quetzalcoatl away.
It is about Spanish conquistadors on horseback wielding a giant Christian cross.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Mexican Civil War.
It was influenced by the First World War.
